title: Roasted Saudi Arabian Pepper Tagine
description: A sweet and gooey one-pot dish featuring roasted peppers, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This Saudi Arabian inspired recipe is cooked to perfection in a donabe vessel.
instructions: Preheat the broiler to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Place the bell peppers on a baking sheet and broil for 30-40 minutes, or until the skin is charred and blistered.
Remove the peppers from the oven and let them cool down, then peel off the skin and chop them into large pieces.
Heat the olive oil in a donabe vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped bell peppers, chicken broth, and honey to the donabe vessel, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired, and serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
